[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 且意 - Conjunction meaning 'furthermore' or 'moreover', used to introduce additional information.
  • 大臣不容欺己 - Literally 'the ministers could not deceive him', suggesting the emperor was aware of the truth or the ministers were honest.
  • 遂携至西市，示于商胡 - '遂' means 'then' or 'thereupon', '携' means 'to carry', '西市' refers to the western market, and '示于商胡' means 'showed to the foreign merchants'.
  • 商胡见之，惊曰 - '商胡' refers to foreign merchants, and '惊曰' means 'were astonished and said'.
  • 上人安得此物 - '上人' is a respectful term for a monk, and '安得此物' means 'how did you get this object?'.
  • 必货此，不违价 - '必货此' means 'you must sell this', and '不违价' means 'at no price'.
  • 僧试求百千，胡人大笑曰 - '僧' means 'monk', '试求' means 'tried to ask for', '百千' is a unit of currency (100,000 coins), and '胡人大笑曰' means 'the foreign merchant laughed and said'.
  • 未也 - Negative response meaning 'not yet' or 'no'.
  • 更极意言之，加至五百千 - '更极意言之' means 'tried even harder to persuade', and '加至五百千' means 'increased to 500,000 coins'.
  • 此直一千万 - '此直' means 'this is worth', and '一千万' means 'one million'.
  • 遂与之 - '遂' means 'then', and '与之' means 'gave it to him'.
  • 僧访其名 - '访' means 'inquired', and '其名' means 'his name'.
  • 常负束藁坐卧于寺两廊下 - '负' means 'carried', '束藁' means 'a bundle of straw', and '坐卧于寺两廊下' means 'sat and lay on the two corridors of the temple'.
  • 不肯住院 - '不肯' means 'would not', and '住院' means 'stay in the lodging'.
  • 经数年 - '经' means 'through', and '数年' means 'several years'.
  • 寺纲维或劝其住房 - '纲维' refers to the head monk or temple administrator, and '劝其住房' means 'urged him to stay in the lodging'.
  • 尔压我耶 - '尔' means 'you', and '压我耶' means 'are you pressing on me?'.
  • 其夕，遂以束藁焚身 - '其夕' means 'that night', '遂' means 'then', '以' means 'with', and '焚身' means 'burned his body'.
  • 至明，唯灰烬耳 - '至明' means 'by morning', and '唯灰烬耳' means 'only ash remained'.
  • 无血之臭 - '无' means 'no', '血' refers to blood and body fluids, and '之臭' means 'of the smell'.
  • 众方知异人 - '众' means 'the crowd', '方' means 'only then', and '知异人' means 'knew he was a strange person'.
  • 遂塑灰为像 - '遂' means 'then', '塑' means 'molded', '灰' means 'ash', and '为像' means 'into a statue'.
  • 今在佛殿上，世号束草师 - '今' means 'now', '在佛殿上' means 'in the Buddha hall', and '世号束草师' means 'the world calls him the Bundled Straw Master'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 僧 - Refers to a Buddhist monk.
  • 商胡 - Refers to foreign merchants, likely from Central Asia or the Middle East, who were active in Chinese markets during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 宝骨 - A mysterious object described as a 'bone of a treasure', which may symbolize spiritual or mystical significance.
  • 束藁 - A bundle of straw, often used in Buddhist rituals or as a symbol of simplicity and asceticism.
  • 束草师 - A title given to the monk who lived in the temple, emphasizing his ascetic lifestyle and the transformation of his remains into a statue.

Proper Nouns

  • 僧 - Monk.
  • 商胡 - Foreign merchants.
  • 宝骨 - Bone of a treasure.
  • 束藁 - Bundle of straw.
  • 束草师 - Bundled Straw Master.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 悉为无事者 - Means 'all are those who have no affairs' or 'all are those who are idle'; suggests a group of people not engaged in practical matters.
  • 句饶方外趣 - Means 'the lines are rich in the interest of the world beyond'; indicates a deep, transcendent quality in the poetry.
  • 静里已驯鸽 - Means 'in the quiet, the tamed pigeons are already there'; indicates a peaceful, serene environment.
  • 斋中亦好鹰 - Means 'in the temple, even the eagles are well cared for'; suggests a harmonious coexistence of different elements.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 锡杖 - A traditional Buddhist staff made of tin, often used by monks.
  • 田衣 - Field clothes, possibly referring to simple, practical clothing worn by monks or farmers.
  • 佛日 - The light of the Buddha, symbolizing enlightenment or spiritual illumination.
  • 魔天 - The demon's sky, symbolizing darkness or evil.
  • 咒 - Incantation, a form of magical or ritual speech.
  • 论处 - Discussion or debate, often used in a formal or scholarly context.

Proper Nouns

  • 梦复 - A name or title, possibly referring to a person or a historical figure.
  • 柯古 - A name or title, possibly referring to a person or a historical figure.
  • 善继 - A name or title, possibly referring to a person or a historical figure.
  • 升上人 - A name or title, possibly referring to a person or a historical figure.

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 續集卷六 寺塔記下 - Title indicating the sixth volume of a continuation of a work on temple and pagoda records. '續集' means 'continuation' or 'sequel,' and '寺塔記' refers to records of temples and pagodas.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虢国夫人 - Yang Guoqin, a concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang and sister of Yang Guifei, known for her influence and beauty.
  • 安禄山 - An Lushan, an infamous general who rebelled against the Tang dynasty, leading to the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 郭暧 - Guo Ai, a Tang dynasty official and驸马, known for his relationship with Princess Jincheng.
  • 太皇太后 - Empress Dowager, a high-ranking female figure in the imperial court.
  • 升平公主 - Princess Jincheng, a Tang dynasty princess, the daughter of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 阿育王 - Emperor Ashoka of India, known for his conversion to Buddhism and the construction of stupas.
  • 司农少卿 - An official title in the Tang dynasty, responsible for agricultural affairs.
  • 关西孔子 - Refers to Confucius, a revered philosopher in Chinese culture, with '关西' indicating the area west of the Guanzhong region.

Proper Nouns

  • 宣阳坊 - The name of a district in Chang'an.
  • 奉慈寺 - The name of the temple.
  • 虢国夫人 - Yang Guoqin.
  • 安禄山 - An Lushan.
  • 郭暧 - Guo Ai.
  • 太皇太后 - Empress Dowager.
  • 升平公主 - Princess Jincheng.
  • 阿育王 - Emperor Ashoka.
  • 司农少卿杨敬之 - Yang Jingzhi, a Tang dynasty official.
  • 明州 - The name of a region, likely referring to present-day Ningbo.

[408]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 光宅坊光宅寺 - Specific location and institution.
  • 本官蒲萄园中禅师影堂 - Originally the shadow hall of a monk in the official grape garden.
  • 师号惠中 - The master's name was Huizhong.
  • 肃宗上元二年征至京师 - In the second year of Shangyuan during the reign of Emperor Suzong, he was summoned to the capital.
  • 杖锡而来 - Coming with a staff and alms bowl, a metaphor for a monk's journey.
  • 斋心已久，副朕虚怀 - Having long fasted and purified his mind, to fill my humble heart, a formal expression of respect.
  • 建中中 - During the Jianzhong era.
  • 竭造曼殊堂 - Built the Manjusri Hall.
  • 将版基于水际 - When laying the foundation near the water.
  • 虑伤生命 - Concerned about harming life.
  • 建三月道场 - Built a three-month meditation hall.
  • 祝一足至多足、无足令他去 - Prayed for all creatures with one foot to many feet, and those without feet to go away.
  • 及掘地至泉，不遇虫蚁 - When digging to the water level, no insects or ants were found.
  • 又以复素过水 - Then passed through the water again.
  • 有虫投一井水中，号护生井 - Insects fell into a well, called the 'Life-Protecting Well.'
  • 至今涸 - It has dried up to this day.
  • 又铸铜蟾为息烟灯 - Also cast a bronze toad for a smoke extinguishing lamp.
  • 天下传之 - Famous throughout the world.
  • 今曼殊院尝转经 - Now the Manjusri Hall often recites sutras.
  • 每赐香 - Often given incense.
  • 宝台甚显 - The treasure terrace is very prominent.
  • 登之，四极眼界 - When climbing it, one can see the four directions clearly.
  • 其上层窗下尉迟画，下层窗下吴道玄画 - Above, the windows have paintings by Yidui, below, paintings by Wu Daoxuan.
  • 皆非其得意也 - None of them are their masterpieces.
  • 丞相韦处厚，自居内廷至相位，每归辄至此塔焚香瞻礼 - Prime Minister Wei Chuhou, from serving in the inner court to becoming prime minister, always came to this pagoda to burn incense and pay homage.
  • 普贤堂，本天后梳洗堂 - The Puxian Hall, originally the dressing room of the Empress.
  • 葡萄垂实，则幸此堂 - When grapes were ripe, the Empress would visit this hall.
  • 今堂中尉迟画颇有奇处 - Now the hall has paintings by Yidui, which are quite unique.
  • 四壁画像及脱皮白骨 - Paintings on the four walls and depictions of skin and bones.
  • 匠意极险 - The craftsmanship is extremely daring.
  • 又变形三魔女，身若出壁 - Also, three demonesses are depicted, their bodies as if emerging from the wall.
  • 又佛圆光，均彩相错乱目 - Also, the halo of the Buddha, with colors mixed and confusing the eyes.
  • 成讲东壁佛座前锦如断古标 - The eastern wall near the Buddha's seat has a brocade that looks like a broken ancient sign.
  • 又左右梵僧及诸蕃往奇，然不及西壁，西壁逼之ВВ然 - Also, on the left and right, there are Indian monks and various foreign figures, but they are not as impressive as the west wall, which is very close.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 光宅寺 - A specific temple in ancient China.
  • 惠中 - The name of a historical monk.
  • 肃宗上元二年 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor Suzong.
  • 建中 - A specific era during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 曼殊堂 - A specific hall dedicated to Manjusri, a bodhisattva.
  • 尉迟画 - Paintings by Yidui, a famous artist.
  • 吴道玄 - Paintings by Wu Daoxuan, another famous artist.
  • 丞相韦处厚 - Prime Minister Wei Chuhou, a historical figure.
  • 普贤堂 - A specific hall dedicated to Puxian, another bodhisattva.
  • 天后 - Refers to the Empress, indicating a historical context.

[409]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 辞 - Title of the text, likely referring to a type of classical Chinese literature, often used for farewell poems or essays.
  • 中禅师影堂连句 - A sequence of couplets in the memorial hall of the Zen Master.
  • 名下固无虚 - There is no emptiness under the name, suggesting that the Zen Master's reputation is well-deserved.
  • 敖曹貌严毅 - 敖曹 is likely a proper name, and '貌严毅' means 'with a stern appearance.'
  • 洞达见空王 - '洞达' means 'thoroughly understand,' and '见空王' refers to seeing the 'King of Emptiness' (a Buddhist concept).
  • 圆融入佛地 - '圆融' means 'perfectly integrated,' and '佛地' means 'the realm of the Buddha.'
  • 一言当要害 - A single word hits the vital point.
  • 忽忽醒诸醉 - '忽忽' means 'suddenly,' and '醒诸醉' means 'awaken the drunkards.'
  • 不动须弥山 - '不动' means 'unmoving,' and '须弥山' refers to Mount Meru, a sacred mountain in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 多方辨无匮 - '多方' means 'in many ways,' and '辨无匮' means 'without limit in argument.'
  • 坦率对万乘 - '坦率' means 'frankly,' and '对万乘' means 'in the presence of the emperor.'
  • 偈答无所避 - '偈' means 'verse,' and '无所避' means 'without evasion.'
  • 尔如毗沙门 - '尔' means 'you,' '如' means 'like,' and '毗沙门' refers to Vaisravana, the guardian god of the north in Buddhist tradition.
  • 外形如脱履 - '外形' means 'outer form,' and '脱履' means 'taking off shoes.'
  • 但以理为量 - '但' means 'only,' '以' means 'with,' '理' means 'reason,' and '为量' means 'as measure.'
  • 不语怪力事 - '不语' means 'do not speak,' '怪力' means 'strange and powerful,' and '事' means 'things.'
  • 木石摧贡高 - '木石' means 'wood and stone,' '摧' means 'break,' and '贡高' means 'arrogance.'
  • 慈悲引贪恚 - '慈悲' means 'compassion,' and '引贪恚' means 'guide greed and anger.'
  • 当时乏支许 - '当时' means 'at that time,' '乏' means 'lack,' and '支许' refers to the philosophers Zhi (支) and Xu (许).
  • 何人契深致 - '何人' means 'who,' '契' means 'understand,' and '深致' means 'deep meaning.'
  • 随宜讵说三 - '随宜' means 'as appropriate,' '讵' means 'how could,' and '说三' means 'say three.'
  • 直下开不二 - '直下' means 'directly,' and '开不二' means 'open the non-duality.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中禅师 - Refers to a Zen Master, likely a historical or legendary figure in Chinese Buddhism.
  • 影堂 - A memorial hall or shrine dedicated to a deceased person, often used in Buddhist contexts.
  • 连句 - A form of poetry where multiple poets contribute lines to form a complete poem.
  • 空王 - A Buddhist term referring to the concept of emptiness (śūnyatā).
  • 佛地 - The realm of the Buddha, a state of enlightenment.
  • 须弥山 - Mount Meru, a sacred mountain in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 毗沙门 - Vaisravana, the guardian god of the north in Buddhist tradition.
  • 支许 - Refers to the philosophers Zhi (支) and Xu (许), who were known for their deep understanding of philosophical texts.
  • 不二 - A Buddhist concept referring to the non-duality of all phenomena.

Proper Nouns

  • 中禅师 - The Zen Master being commemorated.
  • 敖曹 - A proper name, likely a student or follower of the Zen Master.
  • 支许 - Refers to the philosophers Zhi (支) and Xu (许).

[410]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翊善坊保壽寺 - The Bao Shou Temple in Yi Shan Ward, a specific location in Chang'an (modern Xi'an).
  • 天寶九載 - The ninth year of the Tianbao era (750 AD) during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 舍為寺 - To donate as a temple, indicating the conversion of a private residence into a religious site.
  • 舉朝畢至 - The entire court attended, emphasizing the grandeur of the event.
  • 一擊百千 - Describes the loud sound of the bell ringing once, with '百千' being an exaggerated way to indicate a very loud noise.
  • 規其意 - To follow his intention or to comply with his wish.
  • 連擊二十杵 - To ring it twenty times in succession, indicating a series of bell strikes.
  • 經藏閣規構危巧 - Describes the intricate and delicate design of the scripture storage hall.
  • 二塔火珠受十余斛 - Refers to the large size of the fire pearls on the two pagodas, with '受十余斛' indicating they can hold more than ten dou (a unit of volume).
  • 州縣圖三及缣三十獲之 - Three county maps and thirty bolts of silk were used to acquire it, indicating the cost of the painting.
  • 訪於常侍柳公權 - Asked the Grand Attendant Liu Gongquan, seeking expert opinion.
  • 即日進入 - It was taken to the palace on the same day, indicating the immediate action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 翊善坊保壽寺 - Located in Chang'an, the capital of the Tang Dynasty, known for its cultural and religious significance.
  • 天寶九載 - A significant year in the Tang Dynasty, marking the height of the dynasty's prosperity and cultural achievements.
  • 高力士 - A powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty, known for his influence over Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 張萱 - A famous painter of the Tang Dynasty, known for his depictions of court life and landscapes.
  • 《石橋圖》 - A painting by Zhang Xuan, depicting a stone bridge, which is a notable work of Tang Dynasty art.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong, one of the most famous emperors of the Tang Dynasty, known for his cultural patronage and the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 云韶院 - A palace academy in the Tang Dynasty, known for its cultural and educational functions.

Proper Nouns

  • 翊善坊保壽寺 - The Bao Shou Temple in Yi Shan Ward.
  • 高力士 - A prominent eunuch during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 张萱 - A famous painter of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 《石桥图》 - A painting by Zhang Xuan.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 云韶院 - A palace academy in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 河阳从事李涿 - Li Zhuo, an official from Heyang.
  • 僧智增 - Monk Zengzeng.
  • 鬻画人宗牧 - Zong Mu, a picture dealer.
  • 左军 - The Left Army, a military unit in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 柳公权 - Liu Gongquan, a Grand Attendant in the Tang Dynasty.

[411]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先天菩萨帧 - Frame of the Bodhisattva of the先天 (Primordial Heaven/Beginning); a religious image or painting of a bodhisattva associated with primordial or cosmic origins.
  • 本起成都妙积寺 - Originally from the Miaojī Temple in Chengdu; indicates the original location or origin of the painting.
  • 开元初 - At the beginning of the Kaiyuan era; refers to the early years of the Kaiyuan period (713–741) during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 常念大悲咒 - Often recited the Great Compassion Mantra; a Buddhist mantra associated with the Bodhisattva of Compassion (Avalokiteśvara).
  • 奥室立禅 - To meditate in a secluded chamber; '奥室' refers to a secluded or hidden room, often used in monastic or meditative contexts.
  • 尝白魏云 - Once told Hui (the nun) that...; '白' is a classical term meaning to inform or report to someone.
  • 筛灰于庭 - Sifted ash in the courtyard; a ritual or symbolic act, possibly to prepare the ground or to purify the space.
  • 巨迹数尺 - A giant footprint several feet in size; a miraculous or supernatural sign.
  • 轮理成就 - The wheel patterns are complete; refers to the formation of a perfect wheel-like pattern, possibly in the footprint.
  • 随意设色 - To set colors at will; indicates the artist’s attempt to create the image freely.
  • 悉不如意 - None were satisfactory; indicates that the results did not meet expectations.
  • 常合掌仰祝 - Often joined his palms and prayed upward; a gesture of reverence and supplication.
  • 指授之 - Taught him by pointing out; implies a process of instruction or guidance.
  • 近十稔 - Nearly ten years; '稔' is a unit of time, roughly equivalent to a year.
  • 工方毕 - The work was finally completed; indicates the conclusion of the artistic process.
  • 塑先天菩萨凡二百四十二首 - Molded the Bodhisattva of the先天 in total of 242 forms; '首' here may refer to 'forms' or 'images' rather than 'heads.'
  • 首如塔势 - Each form is like a tower in posture; describes the posture or shape of the images as resembling a pagoda or tower.
  • 分臂如意蔓 - Arms are divided into patterns resembling the wish-granting vine (如意); '如意' is a symbolic object in Buddhism and Chinese culture, often associated with wishes or divine power.
  • 榜子 - Labels or inscriptions; likely refers to the inscriptions or labels on the images or the painting.
  • 有一百四十二日鸟树 - One hundred forty-two days of bird tree; unclear, possibly a symbolic or mystical reference. '日鸟树' may be a term from Buddhist or Daoist cosmology.
  • 一凤四翅 - One phoenix with four wings; a mythical bird with an unusual number of wings, possibly symbolic.
  • 水肚树 - Water-bellied tree; a literal translation; likely a symbolic or mythical tree, possibly from Buddhist or Daoist imagery.
  • 所题深怪 - The inscriptions are deeply strange; indicates that the inscriptions are unusual or difficult to interpret.
  • 不可详悉 - Cannot be fully detailed; indicates that the inscriptions are complex or incomplete.
  • 画样凡十五卷 - The painting patterns total fifteen volumes; '画样' refers to the designs or sketches used for the painting.
  • 柳七师者，崔宁之甥 - Master Liu Qi, nephew of Cui Ning; indicates a familial relationship and possibly a patron or associate.
  • 分三卷，往上都流行 - Divided three volumes and spread them upward to the capital; indicates the dissemination of the art to the imperial capital (Chang’an).
  • 时魏奉古为长史 - At that time, Wei Fenggu was the chief clerk (zhǎngshǐ); a title indicating an official position.
  • 进之 - Submitted it; indicates the act of presenting the work to a higher authority.
  • 后因四月八日，赐高力士 - Later, on the eighth day of the fourth lunar month, it was bestowed upon Gao Lishi; a reference to the Buddhist festival of the Buddha’s birthday and the imperial court.
  • 今成都者，是其次本 - The one in Chengdu today is the secondary version; indicates that the Chengdu version is a copy or secondary reproduction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 先天菩萨 - The Bodhisattva of the先天 (Primordial Heaven/Beginning); likely a specific bodhisattva associated with cosmogony or the beginning of the universe in Buddhist or Daoist tradition.
  • 成都妙积寺 - Miaojī Temple in Chengdu; a specific temple in the city of Chengdu, Sichuan province.
  • 大悲咒 - Great Compassion Mantra; a central mantra in Mahayana Buddhism, associated with Avalokiteśvara (the Bodhisattva of Compassion).
  • 奥室 - Secluded room; a common term in Buddhist and Daoist texts to describe a quiet, meditative space.
  • 如意蔓 - Wish-granting vine; a symbolic plant in Chinese and Buddhist culture, representing wishes, blessings, or divine power.
  • 日鸟树 - Tree of the bird of the day; possibly a symbolic or mystical tree, possibly from Buddhist cosmology, though the term is obscure.
